quotes.content: The person, be it gentleman or lady, who has not pleasure in a good novel, must be intolerably stupid.
quotes.author: Jane Austen

quotes.content: A day without sunshine is like, you know, night.
quotes.author: Steve Martin

quotes.content: Anyone who thinks sitting in church can make you a Christian must also think that sitting in a garage can make you a car.
quotes.author: Garrison Keillor

quotes.content: Beauty is in the eye of the beholder and it may be necessary from time to time to give a stupid or misinformed beholder a black eye.
quotes.author: Jim Henson

quotes.content: All you need is love. But a little chocolate now and then doesn't hurt.
quotes.author: Charles M. Schulz

quotes.content: Remember, we're madly in love, so it's all right to kiss me anytime you feel like it.
quotes.author: Suzanne Collins

quotes.content: Some people never go crazy. What truly horrible lives they must lead.
quotes.author: Charles Bukowski

quotes.content: The trouble with having an open mind, of course, is that people will insist on coming along and trying to put things in it.
quotes.author: Terry Pratchett

quotes.content: Think left and think right and think low and think high. Oh, the thinks you can think up if only you try!
quotes.author: Dr. Seuss

quotes.content: The reason I talk to myself is because I’m the only one whose answers I accept.
quotes.author: George Carlin

quotes.content: I am free of all prejudice. I hate everyone equally.
quotes.author: W.C. Fields

quotes.content: A lady's imagination is very rapid; it jumps from admiration to love, from love to matrimony in a moment.
quotes.author: Jane Austen
